headline: India's Rural Water Revolution: 125 Million Homes Gain Tap Access
meta_description: More than 125 million rural households in India now have tap water access, up from just 16.7% in 2019 to 81.7% today.
summary_short: Over 125 million rural Indian households have gained access to tap water since 2019, transforming water access from a luxury to a reality.
body:
More than 125 million rural households in India have gained access to tap water since 2019, marking a dramatic shift in how millions live. In less than five years, the share of homes with running water jumped from 16.7% to 81.7%—a transformation that touches nearly every aspect of daily life for families across the countryside.

The scale of this change is staggering. To reach over a billion people, the expansion required sustained effort and investment at every level. What was once a distant dream for most rural Indians—simply turning on a tap for clean water—has become an everyday reality for the vast majority.

This progress signals a fundamental improvement in living standards across India's rural regions, freeing families from the burden of collecting water and reducing waterborne disease. It represents one of the largest infrastructure pushes in recent years, quietly reshaping how rural India accesses one of life's most essential resources.
quick_facts: 125 million rural Indian households gained tap water since 2019
Water access rose from 16.7% to 81.7% of rural homes
Transformation completed in under five years
